what_it_does: Builds a read-only structural lineage and distance map across session transcripts by reading the forkedFrom provenance field, deriving parent-child edges, and computing time/message/token distances from a shared trunk through the branch point to each fork's tip. It emits a primary lineage.json plus a readable lineage.md narrative.
observations: Uses forkedFrom dictionaries inside jsonl events to classify events as inherited versus own, and picks the latest inherited event by timestamp as the branch point.
Computes distances keyed parent->child, including trunk length, parent post-branch divergence, and child own divergence.
Counts child compactions since branch as the total number of boundaries in the child, including inherited ones.
Markdown output hardcodes trust/author/date/verified metadata rather than deriving it from inputs or runtime.
Reuses scan_session and the private _iter_jsonl helper from sibling modules.
Provides a CLI that writes both JSON and Markdown to an output directory.
standouts: Distance model treats parent and fork as parallel siblings diverging from a shared branch, measuring both sides of the divergence.
Branch detection is purely structural, driven by the forkedFrom provenance field rather than heuristics.
Output follows a data-primary / prose-secondary contract, with lineage.json as the canonical artifact.
conventions: Docstrings reference external vision sections and COMMIT-GRAMMAR.
Type hints and from __future__ import annotations.
Read-only analysis: no mutation of input transcripts.
concerns: Hardcoded markdown provenance block may be stale or misleading for other datasets.
child_compactions_since_branch uses total child boundaries, not just post-branch ones.
Parent post-branch segment selection uses a 5-second tolerance that could include pre-branch segments.
Branch timestamp is taken from child.first_ts, not from the parent event timestamp recorded in forkedFrom.
Uses private functions from other modules, creating tight coupling.
notes: A structural lineage analyzer that turns fork provenance into a distance map and a human-readable narrative of how sessions diverge.
questions: Is the 5-second tolerance in parent post-branch segment selection intentional to handle clock skew or an approximation?
Is the hardcoded markdown metadata a placeholder meant to be replaced per run?
What guarantees that forkedFrom.messageUuid and timestamp are present on every inherited event?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it discovers fork trees from provenance and computes trunk/divergence distances, producing json plus explanatory markdown.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make session forks navigable by quantifying how much shared history each fork carries and how far it has diverged, so downstream tooling can decide where to resume or clone points in time.
novelty: It models a fork not just as a child of a parent but as a parallel sibling, explicitly measuring the parent's continued divergence after the branch alongside the fork's own growth.
contribution: Provides a deterministic, provenance-based lineage builder and distance calculator that produces both a machine-readable map and a human-readable narrative for session fork analysis.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/session_lineage.py builds a structural fork tree and distance map across session jsonl transcripts using forkedFrom provenance, emitting lineage.json and an explanatory lineage.md.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to quantify how sessions share trunk history and diverge after a branch, supporting point-in-time cloning decisions.

symbols.does: Identifies a session's branch point by scanning inherited events and returning parent id, branch uuid, timestamp, and inherited/own counts.
symbols.description: Iterates jsonl events, counts events carrying a forkedFrom dict as inherited and user/assistant events without it as own, and records the latest inherited event by timestamp as the divergence frontier. Returns None for roots.

symbols.does: Builds a node record for one session by combining scan_session summary with branch metadata.
symbols.description: Calls scan_session to obtain per-session metrics, derives the session id from the filename, attaches the branch point from _branch_of, and returns a dictionary containing timestamps, token counts, boundaries, models, segments, and root status.

symbols.does: Constructs the full lineage graph and distance matrix for a list of session paths.
symbols.description: Creates node and edge dictionaries from provenance, then computes per-edge distances including trunk length, parent post-branch divergence, and child own divergence in seconds, days, messages, and output tokens. Returns a dictionary with metadata, nodes, edges, and distances.

symbols.does: Shortens a session id to its first dash-separated segment.
symbols.description: A small formatting helper used in markdown output to make session identifiers more readable; returns the full string if no dash is present.

symbols.does: Renders the lineage data structure into a human-readable markdown report.
symbols.description: Produces a markdown document with a hardcoded provenance header, root summaries, per-branch distance narratives, and an orientation paragraph explaining trunk, branch, and parallel divergence concepts.

symbols.does: Writes lineage.json and lineage.md to an output directory and returns file paths plus counts.
symbols.description: Builds the lineage, ensures the output directory exists, serializes the data to JSON and the markdown narrative to a file, and returns a summary of the generated artifacts.
